entry.title: Billing status uodate in outbound delivery
entry.content:
Hi Guys:

I have one sales order with Qty 5 PCS, ans outbound delivery and PGI wiht 5PCS as well. 1st invoice was issued with 3PCS partially, then when I create 2nd invoice with 2PCS. System show 'The document has already been fully invoiced '.

Actually, the delivery is partial invoiced, I checked the billing status in outbound delivery, is C -fully invoiced.

Please kindly help for this issue.

Thanks.

entry.title: Can the client mantain the data of "Output invoice book" Via SAP APP not via IMG ( CBC)?
entry.content:
Hello,  

考量使用者權限設置的安全考量，客戶希望使用者能經由 APP 維護'銷項發票本'資訊，而不是進到IMG去維護，故想請問可以新增一個APP 作業嗎?謝謝!!

entry.title: Re: How To Combine SAP GUI Scripting And Selenium WebDriver
entry.content:
Hello @Girish2000,

I am not a C# programmer so I modified the PowerShell code above. In line 49 the event name is now Hit, in line 48 and 93 is $Session.ActiveWindow.ElementVisualizationMode set to true and false and in line 76 the output is modified. All works as expected. So basically we can say that it works.

The event is not triggered in your case, I assume that this kind of registration is not correct. Try it with ISapSessionEvents_HitEventHandler.

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Use of Average full time equivalent of Accrual period in Time Management to average the quotas
entry.content:
Hi All,

 

REQUIREMENT: There is a situation where in given time account year example: 1st Jan 2024 till 31st December 2024 there can be several records where employee moves from full time to part time or vice versa. System should automatically average the quotas based on type of employment.

EXAMPLE: Employee A worked as a full-time employee from 1st Jan till 31st March that is FTE=1 , and from 1st April till 31st October worked as part time that is FTE=0.5 and again November and December as Full Time.

CALCULATION :

Employement Type

Months

Number of Calendars days

FTE

No of Working days(Number of calendar days * FTE)

Quota

Full Time

1st Jan - 31st March

91

1

91

20

Part Time

1st Apr-31st October

214

0.5

107

20

Full Time

1st Nov-31st December

61

1

61

20

 

 

366 Days

 

259Days

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Final Calculation= 259/366*20=14.15

 

 

 

 

 

SOLUTION: STEP1

Firstly, we need to use the function Average full time equivalent for Accruable period () and store it in a variable as per the screenshot below:

In the fields mentioned please fill the details as per the requirement here I need the calculation for current account period hence I am using account valid from and account valid until.

STEP2: Use this variable under create time account details where it will be used to calculate the accruals.

STEP3: Also please make sure that under time account type, Please select the fields which will trigger recalculation.

Thanks and Regards,

Suraj

entry.title: Re: Activating valuation split for existing material code
entry.content:
Dear Member,

DMLT (data management and landscape transformation) is a team of professional consultants within SAP. Material split valuation (for existing materials) is part of DMLT service portfolio. Service is subjected to system analysis and customer specific. You can reach out to sap_dmlt_gce@sap.com with your requirement.

You can also visit : https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/support-services/data-management-landscape-transformation/unifications.html

Regards.

entry.title: Re: How to implement a custom entity in the ABAP RESTful Programming Model using remote function mod
entry.content:
Hi Community,

Thanks for the selfless sharing.

Worked for us with another RFC.

Thanks for the detailed blog. 

It really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: 10 Key Updates to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Best Practices in 2H 2024 Release
entry.content:
The second half of 2024 brings exciting updates to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Best Practices, which include enhancements across Employee Central Core, Global Benefits, Position Management, and Contingent Workforce Management. This latest release focuses on enhanced content, better user experience, new validations, and improved workflows. Let’s explore some key new features and how these changes will make the best practices content more relevant to our customers.

1. Reimagined Benefit Enrollment for Pensions

Continuing with the migration of USA Benefits from the legacy enrollment experience to the reimagined enrollment experience, Pension plans are included in the new experience in the 2H 2024 release. This means employees now have an intuitive, user-friendly interface when enrolling in pension plans, helping them make informed decisions about their retirement benefits. This change leaves us with the ‘Allowances’ and ‘Deductible Allowances’ benefits types that are still in the legacy experience—these will be included in the reimagined experience in the coming releases.

2. Employee Central Quick Actions for Employee and Manager Self-Services

Quick actions save time by allowing employees and managers to access frequently needed updates directly. This update introduces quick action templates, including:

  • Change Contract End Date
  • View Location
  • Change Chosen Name

These options allow employees and managers to make straightforward updates in an independent manner.

3. New Validation for New Hire's Age in Germany

A validation has been added for German hires to check whether a new hire is over fourteen years of age. This age validation ensures that organizations can meet their employment age requirements automatically out-of-the-box.

4. Field Criteria to Legal Entity in Position Object

In the Position object, the Cost Center field now includes the field criteria for Legal Entity, allowing cost center data to be filtered by the assigned legal entity. This feature enables more precise data maintenance and makes it easier for organizations to manage cost allocations across different legal entities. This update improves the integrity of HR and internal accounting practices for global companies with multiple legal entities.

5. Changes to Approvers in the Position Workflows

The Position Approval Workflows now incorporate the dynamic role “SFCC Org Design Administrator.” The base object for this dynamic role has changed from ‘Job Information’ to ‘Position,’ meaning organizations can assign dynamic groups to this role more flexibly. This change simplifies the workflows, allowing local approvers to be added as necessary and streamlining the position approval process. This change is an improvement over previous version and ensures that approval workflows align with the organizational design requirements.

6. Beneficiaries for the Latest Enrollment Experience

This enhancement allows the addition of beneficiaries in the new Reimagined Benefits enrollment process for the USA Benefits. With this update, employees can now configure beneficiaries for insurance, pension, and savings plans in real-time as they complete their benefits selections. This integrated approach simplifies the enrollment experience and improves transparency for employees.

7. New Opt-Out Feature

Offering employees the flexibility to opt out of specific benefits can be crucial for tailoring benefits to individual needs. The setting “Allow Waiving of Benefit” at the benefit level is maintained to ensure that employees can now opt out of specific benefits in the Reimagined Benefits Enrollment experience for the USA. This flexibility allows employees to avoid unnecessary benefits that may not suit their needs, offering a more personalized benefits experience.

8. Concurrent Employment Workflow Change

For organizations managing concurrent employees, simplifying workflows is essential to ensure efficient approvals and minimize delays. Previously, concurrent employment creation triggered an approval workflow for the target manager, which could have created bottlenecks. Now, based on the feedback we received, the system only sends a notification to the source manager, streamlining the process and improving efficiency in managing concurrent employment scenarios.

9. Validation for Duplicdate Work Order IDs

Based on the feedback we got, there was a need to identify scenarios where an already-existing Work Order ID was used. With 2H 2024, a new validation triggers an error if an existing Work Order ID is entered for a contingent worker. This update helps prevent duplicate entries, ensuring data accuracy. A post-processing step in the Contingent Workforce Management Quick Guide now includes detailed instructions for business rules to assist implementation partners.

10. Sample Positions Have Country/Region Field Maintained

The Sample Employee Master Data provides demo organizational and employee data for running HR Business Processes in Employee Central. The sample data allows HR teams and implementation partners to test configurations, workflows, and integrations in a realistic environment, reducing the time needed to identify and resolve potential issues before going live. In the 2H 2024 release, we added the country/region data to all the sample positions. This change fixed an issue where some data could not be selected depending on the Country. e.g. Employee Class.

 

PS: This blog post does not cover the Time Management content in SAP Best Practices. You can review the What’s New Viewer for more details.

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate for RISE with SAP: Roadmap Updates & New Content!
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" The 'clean core' is a concept designed to achieve modern, flexible, and cloud-compliant ERPs. For an up-to-date ERP, customers achieve a clean core by integrating and extending the system in a cloud-compliant way, while governing master data and business processes. This approach ensures that when it comes time to upgrade a system, changes can be implemented without significant manual efforts to test and adapt existing structures.  "

 

Thanks for the sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.content:
Dear expert

I upload checked_system.xml via note analyzer, but something is wrong. It shows the message as below
Could you tell me what the root cause is ?

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects
entry.content: What would ve sequence of transports from Devlopment to Test if Apps are published in Build workzone and Those apps are consists of build apps UI/ Build apps backend and build process automation workflow? Which applications need to subscribe or only runtime instance before moving those apps to Test subaccount? CTMS should be tool to move App vesrions .

entry.title: Question regarding Data Source Integration in Content Form Templates
entry.content:
Is it possible to include data from 'View Browser' and custom CDS Views as data sources in Content Form Templates for displaying on forms/documents?
(It's acceptable to follow the flow: Table Data → Custom Logic → Form Data Source)
If possible, please explain the scope of what can be developed.

I will describe the specific requirements and questions below. 

Requirements:

  • Need to detect and print "Reprinted" on documents when they are reprinted
  • Need to detect and print modified areas on documents when they are changed

 

Question: "In the on-premise SAP system, it's possible to:

  1. Print 'Reprinted' on documents using records from NAST (Output Management Table)
  2. Print modified areas on documents using records from CDHDR (Change Document Header Table)

 

How can we utilize these records for document printing in the SAP Public Cloud version?"

Thank you.

entry.title: Whether to use rowspan during cap development
entry.content:
I'm currently developing with CAP and would like to know how to use rowspan in the table header.

Currently, I've implemented the table in an XML file, and the screen appears like the photo provided.

 

 

I would like to change it to look like this using a functionality similar to rowspan . How can I achieve this?

 

 

xml file

<t:Table id="_IDTable" selectionMode="None" rowMode="Auto"
<t:columns >
<t:Column width="50px" >
<Label text="aaaaa" width="100%" textAlign="Center" />
<t:template>
<core:Icon src="{selectMeaValRegMap>status}" decorative="false"/>
</t:template>
</t:Column>
</t:columns>
</t:Table>

entry.title: Issue in Connecting SAP S/4 Hana Private Edition system to Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts!

I am trying to connect our S/4 Hana private edition (Cloud System) to Eclipse, and in eclipse i added ABAP Cloud project and tried connecting with the Fiori URL and I am getting error "Logon to the ABAP service instance has failed". Attaching Screenshot for your reference.

Kindly help.

Thanks in Advance,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: Re: Easy ECC deployment
entry.content:
To help you with an easier deployment of SAP ECC and to analyze how effectively your modules and custom Z-codes are being used, there are a few tools and value accelerators that can make the process smoother and provide the insights you need:

1. SAP Readiness Check

  • What It Does: This tool is great for evaluating your current SAP ECC system before transitioning to S/4HANA. However, it’s also very handy for understanding the usage of your custom Z-codes and active modules in your system.
  • How It Helps:
    • It identifies custom code (including those pesky Z-codes) to show what's in use and what might be outdated or redundant.
    • You’ll get insights on active modules, compatibility issues, and even recommendations on optimization.
    • It provides a clear dashboard to see the big picture at a glance.

2. SAP Signavio Process Intelligence (Process Mining)

  • What It Does: This tool gives you a powerful way to analyze business processes within your SAP ECC system, helping you see what's being used effectively and what's gathering dust.
  • How It Helps:
    • It connects to your SAP ECC data to analyze business processes in real-time.
    • You can identify bottlenecks, underutilized custom developments, and redundant modules.
    • With this level of visibility, you can quickly pinpoint which Z-codes or modules are adding real value and which ones might need a re-think.
    • Use Plug and Gain Approach (Value Accelerator withing Signavio) with Signavio Process Insights and Process Intelligence:
      • This approach offers a quantitative analysis of your existing system and transaction codes.
      • You'll also get a detailed view of process execution, helping you understand how transactions are flowing and which areas are delivering the most value.

3. ABAP Code Inspector and Usage Analytics Tools

  • What It Does: These tools are designed to help you see which Z-codes and custom developments are genuinely adding value and which ones aren’t getting used as much.
  • How It Helps:
    • Tools like the ABAP Call Monitor (SCMON) can track what custom code is actually being executed.
    • Custom Code Lifecycle Management (CCLM) within SAP Solution Manager helps you catalogue and assess your custom objects to see how frequently they’re being used.

4. SAP Solution Manager Usage Analytics

  • What It Does: This provides a broader look at module utilization and helps you track and evaluate the performance of your custom developments.
  • How It Helps:
    • It offers insights into which modules are being used, how often, and how they align with your business goals.
    • You can keep tabs on key business process metrics to ensure you’re getting the most out of your system.

A Few More Tips:

  • If you're thinking about the move to S/4HANA, consider using Fiori apps for a more streamlined experience and better process optimization.
  • You might also find value in reaching out to SAP Enterprise Support Services. They can provide tailored guidance and tools to make sure you’re getting the most out of your current system and future plans.

With these tools and strategies, you'll have a clear view of what’s working, what’s not, and where you can make improvements for maximum impact. Happy optimizing

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager SAP Signavio Process Insights

entry.title: Regarding the Schedule End Time in Schedule Delivery Creation
entry.content:
Application: Schedule Delivery Creation
Component: LE-SHP-GF-JS

Requirement: In the Schedule Delivery Creation app, I want to define the job end time
Question: In the Schedule Delivery Creation app, while we can specify the job start time, is it possible to schedule a job with a time range, such as AM09:00~AM11:00?

Note: On the screen, I could only find the parameter for start time.

I am attaching a screenshot of the start time parameter section below.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Automatic creation/assignment of batch during inbound...
entry.content: try to use MSC1N create batch for your material and check whether it is successful.

entry.title: How to populate columns dynamically in SAP RAP?
entry.content:
Hi All,

Based on the input provided by the user, the column names should be dynamically fetched from the backend and populated in the child entity. The number of columns may vary depending on the user's input."-Is this possible in SAP RAP?

or

If i create some fixed columns in a table.Is there any way to map column names dynamically?

@Andre_Fischer 

Thanks,

Swetha

entry.title: Managing AI Models with SAP AI Core on BTP: Setup to Deployment Using Docker, GitHub & Object Store
entry.content:
Introduction

Managing AI assets in a scalable, standardized way can feel like taming a wild beast—unless you're using SAP AI Core. This service on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides a powerful engine for running AI workflows and model serving workloads, complete with integration capabilities for tools like Docker, GitHub, and Object Store. But enough with the buzzwords—let's dive in and make AI magic happen, one model at a time!

SAP Documentation: What Is SAP AI Core? | SAP Help Portal

 

Architecture Overview

 

To get things rolling, let’s break down the architecture (don’t worry—no complicated blueprints involved).

  • SAP AI Core is your main engine for AI workflows and model serving.
  • SAP AI Launchpad is like a friendly control tower, managing multiple AI runtimes as a SaaS application.
  • AI API? It standardizes managing the AI lifecycle across different runtimes. Simple, right?
  • Git Repository stores your training and serving workflows as templates.
  • Docker Repository? It handles custom Docker images (think bundled code with all dependencies included).
  • Data Storage is where input/output artifacts like training data and models chill out.

 

Initial Setup Steps

Setting up can sound intimidating, but it’s just a series of simple steps (with a few cups of coffee along the way):

  1. Create a Subaccount on AWS (SAP AI Core is currently supported here).
  2. Enable Cloud Foundry and create a space.
  3. Add Service Plans for SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad.
  4. Create Service Instances for both and generate service keys for access.

 

 

Voila! You're ready to connect and roll.

 

Integrating Docker, GitHub, and Object Store

Docker Repository

Here’s where we package our AI code into tidy Docker images. SAP AI Core can fetch these images and ensure all dependencies (yes, even the finicky ones) are ready to go. Think of Docker as the suitcase that fits everything you need for your AI journey.

A Docker repository is essential for storing AI code in the form of Docker images on the cloud. SAP AI Core accesses this repository to retrieve the code. The Docker image ensures that the code is packaged with all necessary dependencies, directory structures, and drivers needed for GPU utilization.

Key Components of the Docker Image:

  1. Machine Learning Code - The main Python script.
  2. Dependencies - Packages required by the ML code, specified in the requirements.txt file.
  3. Dockerfile - Instructions for building the Docker image

Example Dockerfile:

Pro tip: Organizations with more than 250 employees require a paid Docker Desktop subscription. For this reason, many IT teams opt for alternatives like Podman. Also, remember to store Docker credentials using secrets in the AI Launchpad to allow SAP AI Core to pull from private repositories. Just make sure your .dockerconfigjson format is correct—it’ll save a few headaches!

Storing Docker Credentials:

To allow SAP AI Core to pull Docker images from a private repository, storing Docker credentials is necessary:

Add a Docker Registry Secret in AI Launchpad:

Note: SAP AI Core does not verify Docker credentials.

Need to store Docker credentials? Use secrets in AI Launchpad. Pro tip: Double-check your .dockerconfigjson format (it saves headaches later).

 

GitHub Repository

This is where your AI workflows live and thrive. Configuration files in YAML format hold the instructions for SAP AI Core. Connect using a GitHub access token, and you’re set.

Object Store

Managing input/output artifacts like training data and models? Enter the Object Store—think of it as your cloud storage (AWS S3, Azure Blob, Google Cloud Storage, etc.). Adding data artifacts requires tools like Postman or the AI Core SDK; AI Launchpad won’t cut it for this step. What Is Object Store? | SAP Help Portal

 

Building AI Workflows

Training Phase
  1. Prepare Your Python Script (with dependencies).
  2. Create a Docker Image and push it to Docker Hub.
  3. Sync YAML Files to create configurations and deployments in AI Launchpad.
  4. Execute the Workflow and watch as your AI model trains and shines.
Serving Phase

Craft a serving script using tools like Flask. Build, push, configure, and deploy—then get ready to make predictions. Just remember, each deployment URL is unique!

Addressing the Unique Deployment URL Challenge

When you deploy a machine learning model in SAP AI Core, it's exposed via a deployment URL for consumption. By default, each new deployment results in a unique URL. While this allows for flexibility, it can complicate integration with applications that expect a consistent endpoint.

Strategies to Maintain a Static Deployment URL:

  1. Patching Existing Deployments: SAP AI Core offers a feature to update (or "patch") an existing deployment. This means you can modify configurations or update the model without changing the deployment ID, thereby maintaining the same URL. Utilize the AI Launchpad or AI Core SDK to apply patches, ensuring a consistent endpoint for connected applications.

  2. Implementing a Reverse Proxy: Setting up a reverse proxy to route requests from a static URL to the dynamic deployment URL allows applications to interact with a consistent endpoint. This approach requires additional setup and maintenance but ensures consistent access for external systems.

Visual Studio Code Toolkit

For those who like one-stop development, the VS Code Toolkit connects directly to AI Core. Build, configure, and deploy with ease, all within the familiar VS Code interface.

Practical Examples and ML Workflow Management with SAP AI Core

Getting Started with Scripts and Workflows

For practical examples of Python scripts, YAML files, Dockerfiles, and more, you can explore this resource on SAP Developers. Here's a brief overview of key steps to get your AI scripts served via SAP AI Core:

Steps to Deploy a Script using SAP AI Core:

  1. Create a Docker Image: Include your main.py, requirements.txt, and Dockerfile.
  2. Push the Image to Docker Hub.
  3. Connect GitHub to SAP AI Core and create an application from a YAML file.
  4. Create a Configuration in AI Core.
  5. Deploy your application in AI Core.

Training Phase for ML-Flow

Training your machine learning models involves the following:

  1. Obtain or Create Your Python Script from the data scientist.
  2. Understand Your Code: Identify required libraries and data imports.
  3. Create a YAML File (type: WorkflowTemplate) in your GitHub repository. Specify details such as the Docker image name, Docker Hub info, placeholders for cloud storage, input types, etc.
  4. Create an Application in AI Launchpad (or use VS Code) to access the new YAML file.
  5. Synchronize to view your new scenario, including workflow executables and parameters/input artifacts as defined in your YAML.
  6. Upload Training Data to your Cloud Storage if needed.
  7. Modify Your Python Script:
    • Define variables for the training data path in the Docker image (as specified in the YAML file).
    • Specify the model save path.
    • Adjust other YAML-referenced variables.
  8. Save Your Trained Model.
  9. Create a requirements.txt File for listing required libraries.
  10. Create a Dockerfile to package the environment, including placeholders for data storage.

Building and Pushing the Docker Image:

  • Start a Podman Virtual Machine if using Podman as an alternative to Docker Desktop.
  • Navigate to the Directory containing your files (main.py, requirements.txt, Dockerfile).
  • Build the Image: Use podman build --tls-verify=false -t docker.io/<YOUR_DOCKER_USERNAME>/<YOUR_IMAGE_NAME>:<VERSION> . (Note the space and dot at the end.)
  • Push the Image to Docker Hub.

Once ready, create a configuration in AI Launchpad (or VS Code), select your scenario, and create an execution to train the dataset. Your trained model will then be accessible in the Object Store.

Serving Phase for ML-Flow

To serve a trained model, follow these steps:

  1. Create a Serving Python Script:
    • Utilize a framework like Flask, for example, main.py.
    • Create a serving engine using app = Flask(__name__).
    • Define a prediction call with @app.route("/v1/predict", methods=["POST"]).
    • Load request data and the trained model, execute predictions, and return the response.
  2. Create a ServingTemplate YAML File in your GitHub repository, specifying details like your Docker image, Docker Hub, and port.
  3. Create an Application in AI Launchpad (or VS Code) and verify synchronization.
  4. Prepare Necessary Files:
    • requirements.txt for required libraries (e.g., Flask).
    • Dockerfile for packaging the environment.
  5. Build and Push the Docker Image following similar steps as in the training phase.
  6. Create a Configuration and deploy your scenario. An API Endpoint URL will be provided for making predictions.

 

Conclusion

By combining SAP AI Core with Docker, GitHub, and Object Store, you’ve got a powerful toolkit for scalable AI model management. So, whether you're deploying complex ML workflows or serving models faster than you can say “SAP AI Core,” you’re now equipped to handle it all—no sweat (well, maybe a little).

Ready to make some AI magic happen? Let’s go!

entry.title: Information Needed: Cancel Open Quantity Button Behavior Change in Purchase Request (2411 Update)
entry.content:
SAPコミュニティの皆様、こんにちは。

最近の 2411 アップデートに続いて、購入リクエスト機能が変更され、「購入リクエストが完全に注文され、配送されると、[未処理数量のキャンセル] ボタンが自動的に無効になります。」と表示されました。

顧客は購入リクエスト機能を積極的に使用しており、次のことを理解する必要があります。

  1. この変更の詳細な範囲と影響
  2. ボタンが無効になる特定の条件
  3. さまざまなシナリオで予想される動作

どなたか提供していただけますか:

  • 詳細なステップバイステップの検証手順 (例: 1. 購入リクエスト ワーク センターを開く、2. [新規] ボタンをクリックする、など)
  • 可能であれば、この機能を示すスクリーンショット
  • この変更に関する追加の技術文書

この情報は、現在のプロセスへの影響をよりよく理解し、それに応じてユーザーを準備するのに役立ちます。

ご協力をよろしくお願いいたします。

よろしくお願いします

 

https://help.sap.com/whats-new/2e5f185568e744db82065313bb​​bcc6ae?locale=en-US&Business_Area=Supplier+Relationship+Management

entry.title: New Stock Check Behavior in Over-the-Counter Sales: Detailed Information Needed (2411 Update)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Regarding the 2411 update, I need detailed information about the new stock availability check functionality in Over-the-Counter Sales. Specifically, this update note states:

'When creating an Over-the-Counter Sales document, the system now checks the requested quantity of selected products against the available stocks at item level. This functionality applies to Over-the-Counter Sales created manually from the UI and web service.

The check validates if the requested quantity of an item in the Over-the-Counter Sales document is greater than the available stock for that item. If one or more items do not have sufficient stock, the respective line items become inconsistent, and the Invoice button is disabled.

If the Available Stocks field displays no value, there is no stock available in the product.'

Could someone please provide:

  1. Detailed step-by-step test procedures (e.g., 1. Open Over-the-Counter Sales work center, 2. Click New button, etc.)
  2. Screenshots showing:
    • How inconsistent line items are displayed
    • The disabled Invoice button state
    • The Available Stocks field display
  3. Information about:
    • How this affects existing processes
    • Any possible workarounds when stock is insufficient
    • Impact on web service implementations

Your detailed insights would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance

 

https://help.sap.com/whats-new/2e5f185568e744db82065313bbbcc6ae?locale=en-US&Business_Area=Customer+Relationship+Management

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign – What’s New 24.11
entry.content:
I've just posted a couple of questions to the SAP Community regarding the 2411 update features that our customers are interested in. I'd really appreciate your insights on these topics:

1, https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/information-needed-cancel-open-quantity-button-behavior-change-in-purchase/qaq-p/13942800
2, https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/new-stock-check-behavior-in-over-the-counter-sales-detailed-information/qaq-p/13942801

entry.title: Managing AI Models with SAP AI Core on BTP: Setup to Deployment Using Docker, GitHub & Object Store
entry.content:
Introduction

Managing AI assets in a scalable, standardized way can feel like taming a wild beast—unless you're using SAP AI Core. This service on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides a powerful engine for running AI workflows and model serving workloads, complete with integration capabilities for tools like Docker, GitHub, and Object Store. But enough with the buzzwords—let's dive in and make AI magic happen, one model at a time!

SAP Documentation: What Is SAP AI Core? | SAP Help Portal

 

Architecture Overview

 

To get things rolling, let’s break down the architecture (don’t worry—no complicated blueprints involved).

  • SAP AI Core is your main engine for AI workflows and model serving.
  • SAP AI Launchpad is like a friendly control tower, managing multiple AI runtimes as a SaaS application.
  • AI API? It standardizes managing the AI lifecycle across different runtimes. Simple, right?
  • Git Repository stores your training and serving workflows as templates.
  • Docker Repository? It handles custom Docker images (think bundled code with all dependencies included).
  • Data Storage is where input/output artifacts like training data and models chill out.

 

Initial Setup Steps

Setting up can sound intimidating, but it’s just a series of simple steps (with a few cups of coffee along the way):

  1. Create a Subaccount on AWS (SAP AI Core is currently supported here).
  2. Enable Cloud Foundry and create a space.
  3. Add Service Plans for SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad.
  4. Create Service Instances for both and generate service keys for access.

 

 

Voila! You're ready to connect and roll.

 

Integrating Docker, GitHub, and Object Store

Docker Repository

Here’s where we package our AI code into tidy Docker images. SAP AI Core can fetch these images and ensure all dependencies (yes, even the finicky ones) are ready to go. Think of Docker as the suitcase that fits everything you need for your AI journey.

A Docker repository is essential for storing AI code in the form of Docker images on the cloud. SAP AI Core accesses this repository to retrieve the code. The Docker image ensures that the code is packaged with all necessary dependencies, directory structures, and drivers needed for GPU utilization.

Key Components of the Docker Image:

  1. Machine Learning Code - The main Python script.
  2. Dependencies - Packages required by the ML code, specified in the requirements.txt file.
  3. Dockerfile - Instructions for building the Docker image

Example Dockerfile:

Pro tip: Organizations with more than 250 employees require a paid Docker Desktop subscription. For this reason, many IT teams opt for alternatives like Podman. Also, remember to store Docker credentials using secrets in the AI Launchpad to allow SAP AI Core to pull from private repositories. Just make sure your .dockerconfigjson format is correct—it’ll save a few headaches!

Storing Docker Credentials:

To allow SAP AI Core to pull Docker images from a private repository, storing Docker credentials is necessary:

Add a Docker Registry Secret in AI Launchpad:

Note: SAP AI Core does not verify Docker credentials.

Need to store Docker credentials? Use secrets in AI Launchpad. Pro tip: Double-check your .dockerconfigjson format (it saves headaches later).

 

GitHub Repository

This is where your AI workflows live and thrive. Configuration files in YAML format hold the instructions for SAP AI Core. Connect using a GitHub access token, and you’re set.

Object Store

Managing input/output artifacts like training data and models? Enter the Object Store—think of it as your cloud storage (AWS S3, Azure Blob, Google Cloud Storage, etc.). Adding data artifacts requires tools like Postman or the AI Core SDK; AI Launchpad won’t cut it for this step. What Is Object Store? | SAP Help Portal

 

Building AI Workflows

Training Phase
  1. Prepare Your Python Script (with dependencies).
  2. Create a Docker Image and push it to Docker Hub.
  3. Sync YAML Files to create configurations and deployments in AI Launchpad.
  4. Execute the Workflow and watch as your AI model trains and shines.
Serving Phase

Craft a serving script using tools like Flask. Build, push, configure, and deploy—then get ready to make predictions. Just remember, each deployment URL is unique!

Addressing the Unique Deployment URL Challenge

When you deploy a machine learning model in SAP AI Core, it's exposed via a deployment URL for consumption. By default, each new deployment results in a unique URL. While this allows for flexibility, it can complicate integration with applications that expect a consistent endpoint.

Strategies to Maintain a Static Deployment URL:

  1. Patching Existing Deployments: SAP AI Core offers a feature to update (or "patch") an existing deployment. This means you can modify configurations or update the model without changing the deployment ID, thereby maintaining the same URL. Utilize the AI Launchpad or AI Core SDK to apply patches, ensuring a consistent endpoint for connected applications.

  2. Implementing a Reverse Proxy: Setting up a reverse proxy to route requests from a static URL to the dynamic deployment URL allows applications to interact with a consistent endpoint. This approach requires additional setup and maintenance but ensures consistent access for external systems.

Visual Studio Code Toolkit

For those who like one-stop development, the VS Code Toolkit connects directly to AI Core. Build, configure, and deploy with ease, all within the familiar VS Code interface.

Practical Examples and ML Workflow Management with SAP AI Core

Getting Started with Scripts and Workflows

For practical examples of Python scripts, YAML files, Dockerfiles, and more, you can explore this resource on SAP Developers. Here's a brief overview of key steps to get your AI scripts served via SAP AI Core:

Steps to Deploy a Script using SAP AI Core:

  1. Create a Docker Image: Include your main.py, requirements.txt, and Dockerfile.
  2. Push the Image to Docker Hub.
  3. Connect GitHub to SAP AI Core and create an application from a YAML file.
  4. Create a Configuration in AI Core.
  5. Deploy your application in AI Core.

Training Phase for ML-Flow

Training your machine learning models involves the following:

  1. Obtain or Create Your Python Script from the data scientist.
  2. Understand Your Code: Identify required libraries and data imports.
  3. Create a YAML File (type: WorkflowTemplate) in your GitHub repository. Specify details such as the Docker image name, Docker Hub info, placeholders for cloud storage, input types, etc.
  4. Create an Application in AI Launchpad (or use VS Code) to access the new YAML file.
  5. Synchronize to view your new scenario, including workflow executables and parameters/input artifacts as defined in your YAML.
  6. Upload Training Data to your Cloud Storage if needed.
  7. Modify Your Python Script:
    • Define variables for the training data path in the Docker image (as specified in the YAML file).
    • Specify the model save path.
    • Adjust other YAML-referenced variables.
  8. Save Your Trained Model.
  9. Create a requirements.txt File for listing required libraries.
  10. Create a Dockerfile to package the environment, including placeholders for data storage.

Building and Pushing the Docker Image:

  • Start a Podman Virtual Machine if using Podman as an alternative to Docker Desktop.
  • Navigate to the Directory containing your files (main.py, requirements.txt, Dockerfile).
  • Build the Image: Use podman build --tls-verify=false -t docker.io/<YOUR_DOCKER_USERNAME>/<YOUR_IMAGE_NAME>:<VERSION> . (Note the space and dot at the end.)
  • Push the Image to Docker Hub.

Once ready, create a configuration in AI Launchpad (or VS Code), select your scenario, and create an execution to train the dataset. Your trained model will then be accessible in the Object Store.

Serving Phase for ML-Flow

To serve a trained model, follow these steps:

  1. Create a Serving Python Script:
    • Utilize a framework like Flask, for example, main.py.
    • Create a serving engine using app = Flask(__name__).
    • Define a prediction call with @app.route("/v1/predict", methods=["POST"]).
    • Load request data and the trained model, execute predictions, and return the response.
  2. Create a ServingTemplate YAML File in your GitHub repository, specifying details like your Docker image, Docker Hub, and port.
  3. Create an Application in AI Launchpad (or VS Code) and verify synchronization.
  4. Prepare Necessary Files:
    • requirements.txt for required libraries (e.g., Flask).
    • Dockerfile for packaging the environment.
  5. Build and Push the Docker Image following similar steps as in the training phase.
  6. Create a Configuration and deploy your scenario. An API Endpoint URL will be provided for making predictions.

 

Conclusion

By combining SAP AI Core with Docker, GitHub, and Object Store, you’ve got a powerful toolkit for scalable AI model management. So, whether you're deploying complex ML workflows or serving models faster than you can say “SAP AI Core,” you’re now equipped to handle it all—no sweat (well, maybe a little).

Ready to make some AI magic happen? Let’s go!

entry.title: Rf scanning QR codes in warehouse processes
entry.content: Is het possible in public cloud to scan QR codes into the RF menu? And how can we arrange that?

entry.title: Facing the same issue today
entry.content: BTP not working

entry.title: Stock not moving into In-transit storage location at O4G1 - Loading confirmation
entry.content:
Upon loading confirmation, stock is not moving to in-transit storage location except there is stock in that in-transit storage location. I can't seem to figure out why.

1. In my bulk shipment type 'bypass intransit storage location at loading flag is set' 

2. In transit posting group is set to TU

3. Fixed storage in in-transit storage location is set to 1 for TU in in-transit storage location determination.

What am I missing at configuration?

entry.title: Assistance Needed to Enable Highlighted Checkbox
entry.content:
I am writing to request assistance with enabling the checkbox highlighted in the attached screenshot. I believe this issue might be resolved through a BADI implementation, but I would appreciate your suggestions and guidance on how to proceed.

Please find the screenshot attached for your reference.

Sure, I can help you structure that description:

I am writing to request assistance with enabling the checkbox highlighted in the attached screenshot.

What I tried: I attempted to enable the checkbox through the standard configuration settings but was unable to locate the specific option required.

What I expected to happen: I expected to find an option within the configuration settings that would allow me to enable the checkbox directly.

What actually resulted: Despite searching through various configuration menus, I could not find any setting to enable the checkbox. As a result, the checkbox remains disabled and the related functionality is not accessible.

Additional context: I believe this issue might be resolved through a BADI implementation, but I would appreciate your suggestions and guidance on how to proceed.

entry.title: Re: Subsequent free of charge delivery
entry.content: If i Change to copying requirements to 303 that means always an item then it is working

entry.title: Re: SAP integration suite data in excel sheet
entry.content:
Hi Suhail,

You can try building an extractor iflow using the existing cloud integration platform APIs to generate the data you are after and populate them in a spreadsheet.

Cloud Integration APIs - https://api.sap.com/package/CloudIntegrationAPI/odata
You can query specifically the Integration Content apis for existing iflows and configurations.

I built something similar last year to generate an interface catalogue and you can get some ideas from it and add your requirements on top of it. 

Refer - https://github.com/nageshwarrao19/CI_AutomatedInterfaceCatalogue

entry.title: Re: How to create a model in SAC joining multiple tables
entry.content: This feature is only available for classic model at the moment. For new model, it's currently planned for 2025 QRC2: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=67838200100800006884&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=6EAE8B28C5D91EDB899A5161F68CC0EE

entry.title: SAP BTP Service Consumption Model creation - Error when creating model for OData V2 service
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I'm trying to create a service consumption model in BTP environment to consume an OData service in our SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud system. I followed the approaches described here: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/generating-proxies-for-remote-odata-service?version=sap_btp

The .edmx file I downloaded from here: https://api.sap.com/api/API_CHANGEMASTER_0002/overview

Then I use that .edmx file to create the service consumption model and it returned error "This feature is only supported for proxy models for OData V2 services."

But the OData service is clearly stated as OData V2 when I look at it in SAP API Hub (as per above link).

May I ask what can be the reason here and how to fix it?

Thanks and regards,

Tan Diep Thanh

entry.title: How to Change the Components quantity of alternative items in Production Order Using BAPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

Now i want change the quantities of all components In Production Order. I tried with FM CO_XT_COMPONENT_CHANGE,but it doesn‘t work for alternative items.

is there any BAPI or function Module to achieve this requirement.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Option to trigger email for Usage Decision rejection...
entry.content: Dear Markus, Thank you for your advise. kind regards, Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: My SAP Certification on Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
I'm excited to share that I've recently achieved the SAP Certified Associate - SAP Fiori Application Developer certification. This accomplishment wouldn't have been possible without the invaluable support of the Digital Skills Initiative and the guidance of @David_Chaviano .

As an SAP Fiori Consultant working in a startup, I've always been driven to advance my career. While on-site experience is invaluable, certification is a crucial step in gaining recognition from top-tier organizations.

The Digital Skills Initiative provided me with the opportunity to access essential learning resources on SAP Learning Hub. By dedicating time to mastering new topics and completing the required coursework, I was able to secure a redemption code for a free certification attempt.

I encourage all aspiring SAP professionals and newcomers to take advantage of such initiatives. With hard work and perseverance, you can overcome challenges and achieve your career goals.

And if anyone got certification with help of Digital Skills Initiative, they are giving one more free attempt for inspiring newcomers in our SAP COMMUNITY.
Hope if I got one more attempt, I would utilize to enhance my skill over SAP modules.

#SAPCertification #DigitalSkillsInitiative #SAPFiori #CareerGrowth #SAP

entry.title: Re: Change a dataset in a SAC story and to keep the story...
entry.content:
In general, optimized design experience story supports replace model. But since Dataset is no longer a recommended object and to be replaced by new model type, replace dataset with another dataset is NOT supported. Instead, you can replace a dataset with a new model and keep the story structure. 

Please read more from below resources: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/replace-a-model-in-the-optimized-unified-experience/ba-p/13624484

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/8ccad51728324559aac312c2e1d72ea5.html

entry.title: Re: ISSUE - Automatic Payment Program
entry.content: It's 4 years on and this still helped me fix same issue! Thanks

entry.title: Re: Assignment profile stopped executing after I removed...
entry.content: If you make any change to an AP and Save you need to Execute the changes and Update User Assignments - then the Automatic Process will kick in

entry.title: Employee agreements in EC
entry.content:
Currently, when my customer transfers employees in EC or updates employee pay conditions, they need to re-issue a new contract. They use Spinifex Strato for initial contract generation in SAP Recruiting. To re-generate a contract, they create a Job Requisition and move the internal employee through the Recruiting process.

It seems that to streamline this process, we may need an additional Strato solution for contract generation directly in EC. However, I believe other companies have faced similar challenges and might have identified more efficient ways to generate employee agreements within EC.

Are there potential options for generating employee agreements in EC?

entry.title: Re: Change a dataset in a SAC story and to keep the story...
entry.content:
hi,

what u mean exactly with "new model type"?

How can i change my existing dataset into a "new model type" for my existing story?

br Oliver

entry.title: CAP Multitenancy with Objectstore
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community

I would like to use a CAP application in Java for multitenancy. Apart from the HANA DB HDI container creation feature, I would also like to create objectstore instances for each tenant upon subscription.

Is there a way to achieve this by "intercepting" the request when the subscribe endpoint is called?

/-/cds/deployment/subscribe

Also, am I able to achieve this using CAP without having to implement my own Service Manager utility class? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: No visible border for grid table in MDK
entry.content: @Sourabh1  As Bill already mentioned, there is no column separator option in the Grid Table. If this is a feature you need for your mobile app, please submit a feature request via this link https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279, and we will be happy to review and prioritize it accordingly.

entry.title: Re: Change a dataset in a SAC story and to keep the story...
entry.content: When you create model from model, the default one is with 'new model type'. For how to replace, please read through the blog.

entry.title: Re: Sign the XML content with PKCS7 signer in CPI
entry.content:
Hello Nisha,
Hope you are doing well..!

I Have similar requirement where i need to sign the <Body> node and its child using key store certificate, convert to base64 and then place it under singautreValue tag like below before GZip.

could share me how you achieve your requirement so that i can simulate it with mine 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<Message>

  <Header>

    <Sender>TEST</Sender>

    <Receiver>TEST</Receiver>

    <MessageType>TEST</MessageType>

    <MessageDescription>TEST</MessageDescription>

    <TimeStamp>2024-11-19T11:27:31</TimeStamp>

  </Header>

  <Body>

    <PayrollMessage>

      <PayrollMessageRef>TEST</PayrollMessageRef>

      <PayrollMessageType>TEST</PayrollMessageType>

      <PayrollTransactionCount>1</PayrollTransactionCount>

      <PayrollTransctionAmount>00000.00</PayrollTransctionAmount>

      <PayrollTransaction>

        <SequenceNum>00000</SequenceNum>

        <TransactionData>:20:43733

          :32A:241119XXX0000

          :50:TEST

          :52A:TEST

          :53B:/0000000000

          :57A:TEST

          :59:/00000000000000000000

          TEST

           CITY

          :70:TEST

          :72:000000

          00000,00

          0000,00

          00000,00

          0,00</TransactionData>

        </PayrollTransaction>

      </PayrollMessage>

    </Body>

    <Signature>

      <SignatureValue>MIAGCSqGSIb3DQEHAqCAMIACAQExDzANBglghkgBZQMEAgEFADCABgkqhkiG9w0B==</SignatureValue>

    </Signature>

  </Message>

entry.title: Re: http status 404 not found when login SLD
entry.content: the SAP note is not relevant, we've found out the issue due to the b1c_extAddress.conf in the proxy server mapped to wrong folder, we will need to change the root controlcenter folder to match the nginx installation folder. issue is resolved.

entry.title: since SAP Update it´s not possible to export data as a text file with tcode IW37n
entry.content: since SAP Update it´s not possible to export data as a text file with tcode IW37Nbefore SAP updateafter SAP update

entry.title: Re: How to perform looping in SAP PaPM cloud based on a c...
entry.content:
Hi Hanna,

We have a community blogpost that might help your concern above by taking advantage of the "Advanced Tab" part of the View/Iterative View Function. You may refer to the blogpost below:

View Function: Advanced Tab

As an additional information on View function, you may refer here: SAP PaPM: Application Help: View

To know more about SAP Profitability and Performance Management - Standard Model, please visit our Application Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_PROFITABILITY_PERFORMANCE_MANAGEMENT_CLOUD/56471df1959f4cfd9e3bf7a6d2d5be42/56a0a0a1d8b10f63e10000000a42189c.html

Thanks and regards,

Gene

entry.title: Re: SAP API Management - OAuth 2.0 authentication with Su...
entry.content:
Hi Faisal, 

Keeping my answers short. 

You are right that the policy template my colleague referred to in the blog is now deprecated. Please stay tuned, I'm publishing an updated policy template soon. The new approach will use SAML Assertions generated and signed by API management as documented here. 

Meanwhile, pls note that today we've published a new template that uses SAMBearerAssertion approach directly by using Microsoft EntraID as the identity provider if SuccessFactors is configured so. A blog for this approach will be out soon too. 

Coming to your questions :

1. API Management uses TLSv1.3 (if client can negotiate), else fallback is TLSv1.2. You can easily verify this in a tool like curl.

2. The current approach does not act as an IDP. If you closely look at the sequence, it just invokes the /oauth/idp endpoint and directly gets the SAMLToken from SFSF.

entry.title: Re: Billing status uodate in outbound delivery
entry.content: Hi, please check your copy control settings in transaction VTFL

entry.title: How to set different watermarks for D/T/P system?
entry.content:
Hi SAP，

I used the master template. The print template is modified from D and transferred to P. I only open the form template using ALD and see the watermark field. If I adjust the watermark value in ALD, this will also be transferred to the P system. But I don't want to be the same as the watermark in D system. How do I set this?

my system is S4HANA Cloud public edition.

Thanks,

Lingling

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements preview not displaying cds view fields
entry.content:
I guess you need to annotate the fields of the CDS view to display them in the app.

This can be done with: 

 

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]

entry.title: Applications, Single and Composite roles are not displayed in IAG Access request
entry.content:
Hi All,

While trying to raise a Access request in IAG, no single role, composite role is appearing to be selected. Also, if a user has a application and want to add more roles for the same application, then the same does  not appear . 

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Is RFC connection used in SAP Public Cloud ?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Is RFC still used in SAP Public Cloud ? When i’m trying to access AFO using SAP public Cloud i received below screenshot error. And after adding Business Catalog: SAP_BW_BC_AOF_PC the issue was resolved 

But wanted to understand the reason behind the error message ?

entry.title: Re: Revenue Recognition(Service Order with WBS assigned)
entry.content:
Hi Soma,

in case of service orders, the revenue recognition for Service Documents will apply, as the WBS element account assignment in service orders are only statistical. Please refer to my blog on the topic for further details.

Warm regards
Varun

entry.title: On-stack Clean Core Extensibility: Plain and Simple
entry.content:
One of the things our team has been told is that "We have to decide for ourselves what is clean and what is not". This has proven to be very unhelpful and not factual.

The truth is: there is no mystery as to what constitutes clean extensibility. SAP is actually surprisingly clear on this topic.

Consider the following diagram, which you've probably seen before:

Here we are considering primarily the on-stack section.

What are the tiers?

The definition of Tier 1 is all your developments that only use only released-for-cloud objects, which makes those objects clean and therefore upgrade-safe.

(Objects that are released for cloud are guaranteed by SAP to have a stable interface, hence anything that uses them is guaranteed not to break in an upgrade).

Tier 2 is just a way for you to mitigate the cases where SAP may not have provided a released object, so you create a (temporary) wrapper as a workaround.

So, for example, creating a table that only refers to released data elements, for example, is tier 1.

Creating a table that uses data elements that are not released would be tier 2. (Which is a bad example, because you would in that case probably just create the data elements yourself).

Just creating a table or other object does not suggest Tier 1 or Tier 2; it's whether the object uses released objects or not that decides in which tier it is.

A better example is if you create a CDS view: There, it is more likely that you might have to rely on some view of SAP that is not released. In that case, the recommendation is to create a wrapper, mark that as released for cloud, and use that in your CDS view. That way, your view remains Tier 1 clean, but the wrapper is in Tier 2.

Then, if and when SAP releases something you can use to replace your tier 2 object, you retire your wrapper  and swap it out with the released object.

Using this approach, you reduce the risk that you are creating objects that might cause issues with upgrades.

Tier 3 covers everything that SAP says you must not use, like Dynpro, WebDynpro, unreleased BAdIs, etc. But this is anyway more applicable to people who are migrating developments from an old system, such as in a brownfield scenario. In a greenfield scenario, by contrast, you will most likely avoid creating such developments to begin with. In a brownfield scenario, the idea is to progressively retire all your Tier 3 developments and replace them with Tier 1.

In the case of side-by-side extensibility, where you operate outside of the S/4 system or "off-stack", you can only consume released APIs anyway, which means there is no risk of breaking something in an upgrade, so that is clean by default.

What a released-for-cloud object is

To be clear, this is what we are talking about:

This is what you see when you go to the API State properties of an object that is released for cloud development.

When you make use of the "ABAP for Cloud Development" language version (a.k.a. "ABAP Cloud"), the compiler checks whether the objects you use have been released for cloud and will prevent you from compiling otherwise.

This prevents you from accidentally incorporating non-released objects in your code.

You can tell if an object uses this version of ABAP in the properties of the object:

If you set this language version on a package, objects created in that package will by default also use it, but you can override this per object to use the "Standard ABAP" language version, which would be the case for a wrapper object.

How this ties in with AEM (SAP Application Extension Methodology)

In short, it doesn't, really.

This is another rabbit hole we've been led down, believing that AEM is somehow supposed to help us identify what is clean core. But that is not its purpose.

AEM is a methodology that helps formulate an approach to deciding what technologies to use to fulfil a requirement. But the assumption is that you already know what is clean and what is not, and that is what I try to convey in simple terms above.

entry.title: Re: Missing F110 Payments in BNK_MONI
entry.content:
Hi,

After running the payment program F110, you need to run the transaction FBPM1 to merge the payments and generate the batch. Then, run the transaction BNK_MONIP to check if the batch has been properly created.

The standard process:
1. Transaction BNK_MONIP to check whether a merge has occurred. Red traffic light: The merge has occurred, but the system did not create a batch.Then, use transaction SLG1 and the object 'FBPM' to check the application log and use transaction SM37 to check the jobs with the identifier PAY_BTC*.

2.Solved the problem identified on the job log  and then, you can use the report RBNK_PAYM_SCHEDULE to trigger the batch creation again.

Check also the SAP FAQ note: 1577912 and KBA 2775848 - BCM: Batch is not created.

Hope this help

Best regards,

Mahira

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps for SAP Business One, the next-gen Development Environment
entry.content:
Hi @Darius,

According to exercises documents, I am created a new page to load Purchase Order from SAP but the Page Parameter (don't have Number type) so I can not assign value to DocEntry to load the details data.

Can we do another way to convert from text to number or any template to share with me?

Thank you so much.

Tam

entry.title: remote enabled function module to read material configuration on plant level
entry.content:
Hi,

I am looking for a "remote enabled function module to read material configuration" on plant level

to read this configuration with our "Data Service" server, like in SAP over Tx MD04.

I found one ( CUCB_GET_VALUES_FROM_INST_RFC ), but this produce an error in our ERP system.

Or is there a way, to read the configuration out from tables?

Thx

Holger

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign - State Wise GST Configuration
entry.content:
• Dear @prathibha_b  and Team,

                        We are from Tamilnadu in India , with our registered head office in Tamil Nadu. We have more than 20 client projects in 15 different states, including Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, Maharasta, and Delhi. We have multiple GST number based on state-wise ,and we maintain state-wise GST numbers for our accounts receivable and accounts payable processes. This is our business scenario.

How to capture this business scenario in SAP Business ByDesign in from the Organization chart level: 

As per our requirement:

Head Office (Tamil Nadu): We use the Tamil Nadu GST number for accounts receivable and accounts payable.
Customer: For project-related transactions.
Supplier: For material-related transactions.
Service: For service-related transactions, such as vehicle rent and room rent

Branch Office & Multiple Project Locations:
Kerala: We use the Kerala GST number for accounts receivable and accounts payable.
Customer: For project-related transactions.
Supplier: For material-related transactions.
Service: For service-related transactions, such as vehicle rent and room rent

Andhra: We use the Andhra GST number for accounts receivable and accounts payable.
Customer: For project-related transactions.
Supplier: For material-related transactions.
Service: For service-related transactions, such as vehicle rent and room rent

Maharashtra: We use the Maharashtra GST number for accounts receivable and accounts payable.
Customer: For project-related transactions.
Supplier: For material-related transactions.
Service: For service-related transactions, such as vehicle rent and room rent

we are loking for your valuble support 

Thanks & Regards 

C.Vijaykannan

9345922099

vijaykannan@live.com

Hello,

the dates for 2411 have already been updated and can be seen soon. 

Regards, Andreas

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements preview not displaying cds view fields
entry.content: Hi @MBartsch71 , I have already tried this but have not noticed a change in the fields displayed

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects
entry.content:
Not sure exactly what you are asking but I will try to summarize as best I can:

  • You would have 3 BTP subaccounts, each with SAP Build
  • You would set up CTMS for those 3 tenants
  • You would use transport to move apps/processes from dev to test to prod
  • On each tenant you would still have to build and deploy, as best I can understand. The transport manages the design artifacts, again as best as I understand

2 notes:

  • From what I can see on my system, there currently is no transport for VCF/Apps backend -- I could not find a way to create versioning or do transport. Perhaps this is on the roadmap.
  • This session was about setting up rules for who can create and transport projects, but was not about CTMS or how to do transport.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect download link for 32 bit Crystal for visual...
entry.content: Interesting! That page that you show is not the one I downloaded from yesterday? Could there be more than one page that links. Sadly I did not take note of the URL of the origin page! Let me see if I can find it!

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh - limit of 32767 reached

entry.title: India Business: Keeping Backup of books of Account Leveraging SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Enhanced Record-Keeping Regulations for Indian Businesses

As of August 5, 2022, Indian businesses must adhere to new record-keeping regulations introduced by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). These regulations, outlined in the Companies (Accounts) Fourth Amendment Rules, 2022, mandate that companies ensure their electronic records are accessible within India at all times. Additionally, businesses are required to maintain a daily backup of their books of account data within the country.

Key Requirements

  • Accessibility: Electronic records must be accessible within India at all times.
  • Daily Backup: A daily backup of books of account data must be stored within India.

Leveraging SAP S/4HANA for Compliance

For businesses using SAP S/4HANA, compliance can be streamlined by utilizing the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) statutory reporting solution. This tool helps users extract the necessary books of account data from their SAP S/4HANA systems, ensuring they meet the new regulatory requirements.

Guidance and Support

For detailed guidance on how to implement these changes, SAP has provided SAP Note 3443920 .

Important Note: Please be aware that the information provided here is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal or regulatory advice. Businesses should consult with their legal advisors to ensure full compliance with the new regulations.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to reach out. We're here to help you navigate these new requirements smoothly.

Karthiheyini Subbiah

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski,

I assigned these roles to Default Identity provider.

And the inbox is got because the other user that I used in Recipient User also subscribed "SAP Build Process Automation" service.

This inbox is SAP Build's Inbox. Open form the yellow highlighted icon.

entry.title: Re: WBS Element to Control Budgeting(Capex)
entry.content: Hello, As I know the account number is only statistical. (although it's mandatory) You can input any account. Please test and check. Best Regards, Owen

entry.title: Outbound Delivery show error msg: VL063
entry.content:
Hi All,

Please help, 

The current outstanding sales quantity is 195,000, and an over-delivery tolerance of 10% has been set.

However, the delivery quantity in DN can only be assigned a maximum of 142,000. and setting the quantity to 142,001 will result in a error message VL063-Overdelivery tolerance of 10% has been exceeded.

Can anyone help?

Thanks All !!

Or why a user would have to do anything to receive the role? I think it's best to better understand how BTP works a little better ... happy o assist

entry.title: c4c v2 - How do we get the historical changes done to Opportunity in REST service?
entry.content:
Hello community,

We are looking for historical changes made to the Opportunity from the REST service available Overview | Opportunity Service | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

We had the entity to retrieve the historical changes in v1. Refer to this OData SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Can someone please help to understand how to retrieve the history in REST service? I came to know that the REST service has the attribute called "snapshots", but it never returns when the request is made.

Regards,
Pratik. 

C4C Sales C4C Service C4C Analytics

Siva

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski,

Normally, the work of admin is

1. they can add new users in the system

2. they can decide which user can access which part of the system

3. they can add access to other user as new admin, etc...

 

So, I would like to know the admin role of user in SAP Build Process Automation service is same like other?

And as I checked,

1. I created one approval workflow and I assigned myself as Admin role by assigning "Administrator" role in BTP account.

2. I can access the approval form workflow and can also able to access Inbox that can decide "Approve" or "Reject" the approval form.

3. I added new users in Cloud Identity Service and these users are also able to access the form link directly by using their own user and password.

4. I assigned the ProcessAutomationAdmin, ProcessAutomationParticipant roles to the new user accounts from my BTP account.

5. But, they cannot access the Inbox of SAP Build automatically although I assigned the required roles to these user accounts.

6. I needed to login the other new user BTP account and need to assign the required roles.

7. After then, that new user account also access the Inbox of SAP Build. 

8. I updated the Recipient User of Workflow as the new user mail. But the approval request of "Approve" or "Reject" is not shown in Inbox of new user.

 

I checked the documentation about user assignment provided by SAP and it is hard to understood for me, how the User Role assignment and Admin role works in SAP Build Process Automation.

So, I am checking that I miss some steps (or) do something wrong by asking questions in community blog.

Could you please point out for me if I understood wrongly?

entry.title: Externalized Parameters are not displayed in the configuration view
entry.content:
Hey Community,

I would like to create parameters that can be configured. The background is as follows. When I deploy an integration flow from the test system to the production system, I no longer want to have to make changes within the iFlow. It should be sufficient to adjust the configurable parameters.

In this test scenario, I have created the two externalized parameters "Test1" & "Test2".

My expectation would now be that I have the possibility to set appropriate values for these parameters via the "configure" action of the iFlow. However, in the configure view, for some inexplicable reason, only the last added parameter is available to me.

Does anyone have an idea why this could be? I have already tested the classics such as reloading the page or clearing the brwoser cache and these can be ruled out.

I am grateful for any advice.
Greetings Marc

entry.title: Re: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content: I also tried to look in the documentation but couldn't find anything. I would like to follow this thread if anyone or SAP has a suggestion.

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content: 2 things I'm confused about: How did the other user log into SAP Build (meaning which IDP)? When you say the other user "subscribed" to process automation, hat is confusing, because "subscribing to a service" is done by the admin when they essentially install the service on the subaccount, so what does it the user "subscribed ... o the service"?

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements preview not displaying cds view fields
entry.content: After some testing, I found that activating the view via the SAP GUI as well as the @EndUserText.Label annotation seemed to help with the field names.

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski,

We have 2 emails like user1@company.com and user2@company.com.

And we created BTP account for both email.

So, user1@company.com subscribed to BTP account and has its own subaccount.

And also, user2@company.com subscribed to BTP account and has its own subaccount too.

 

The workflow is created by user1@company.com and we would like to set many approvers in our workflow.

eg. if amount > 100 - approval of user1@company.com will be needed.

      if amount > 200 - approval of both user1@company.com and user2@company.com will be needed.

So, we want to give Inbox and Permission of "Approve" or "Reject" access to both users.

 

And these access are ok for user1@company.com who is created the workflow. But for the user2@company.com, we don't unsure how to give these access.

entry.title: Re: Extend Validity of E-Waybill
entry.content:
You can refer to the Electronic Waybill guide on the SAP help portal for complete implementation and configuration of the solution.

For extending electronic waybill validity, see Extend Electronic Waybill Validity.

We recommend you to implement the new development (See Electronic Waybills) that automatically provides the file to submit based on the submitted electronic documents as compared to the old development (See Electronic Waybills (Legacy)) that requires manual efforts and has a reduced scope.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 and Concur-Integration
entry.content:
Dear Afzal,

please DON'T deinstall the CTE_* add-ons. 
An upgrade from SAP ECC to S/4HANA 2020 upwards is supported and the add-ons are automatically transformed into the S/4HANA Core Source Code. 
If you would uninstall the add-ons you would loose all your data, which could lead to business issues. 

The corresponding note for the SAP Concur Integration is https://me.sap.com/notes/3233748.
The following info is contained in there:
Starting with release S/4HANA 2020 your system will automatically receive the newest software version, since the integration functionality will be delivered as a core functionality and not as add-on anymore.
There are no additional steps required before or after upgrade when upgrading from the add-ons to the releases S/4HANA 2020 and higher. 
Best regards,
Mathias

entry.title: SAP Code-Jam Request in Dubai
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Greetings of the day!

We would like to kindly request a SAP Code Jam event to be hosted at the Bosch office in Dubai. We anticipate an audience of approximately 30-35 participants and would like to extend an open invitation to all interested individuals.

The topic we are particularly interested in is:
With SAP Build: Create Apps, Processes, and Business Sites Without Coding.

We are aiming to organize this event in December 2024, with the specific date to be decided based on the mutual availability of your team and ours.

We would greatly appreciate your consideration and look forward to hearing from you at your earliest convenience.

--
Warm Regards,
Harish Vatsa

entry.title: Re: Time Sheet Draft and submit
entry.content:

2901734 - How to use "save as draft" functionality in My Timesheet Version 3
